findings.description: The NC Energy, Mineral & Land Resources logo/graphic is present in the HTML but appears smaller and positioned differently relative to the header text.
findings.evidence: PDF shows the logo prominently in the upper left with header text on the right. HTML shows a similar arrangement but the logo appears as a smaller rendered version.

findings.description: The To/From/Subject fields in the HTML are rendered without the tabular indentation structure of the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF has clear label-value alignment with indentation for the To, From, and Subject fields. HTML renders them as sequential lines without the same structured spacing.

findings.description: The MEMORANDUM heading in the HTML has a horizontal rule underneath it, which the PDF shows as underlined text without a full-width rule.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'MEMORANDUM' is underlined centered text. HTML: 'MEMORANDUM' has a full-width horizontal rule beneath it.

findings.description: The outline items (I through IV) in the HTML lack the same indented columnar alignment as the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF shows Roman numerals aligned with content indented to the right. HTML shows them as a simple list with less structured alignment.

findings.description: Bullet points in the HTML use filled circles (disc style) while the PDF uses open/hollow circles for the CAMA process list.
findings.evidence: PDF page 3: hollow bullet points for the CAMA process items. HTML: filled disc bullets.

findings.description: The MEMORANDUM title is styled as h1 with underline and center alignment, which is acceptable. However, the section headings (I, II, III, IV) are rendered as h2 elements, which is appropriate given h1 is used for the document title. No hierarchy skip issues found.
findings.evidence: h1: MEMORANDUM, h2: I. History/Background, h2: II. March 10, 2016 Public Meeting and Oral Comments Summary, h2: III. Written Public Comment Summary, h2: IV. Attachments

findings.description: The first image has a reasonable alt text describing the NC DEQ logo. However, three subsequent images on pages 2-4 have empty alt text attributes (alt=""), which are likely page-level decorative images or repeated headers. This is minor since the images appear to be repeated header/logo images.
findings.evidence: First img: alt="NC Department of Environmental Quality Logo"; Pages 2-4 imgs: alt=""

findings.description: The HTML has a broken list structure where the bulleted list from the oral comments section (Section II) is split across pages 2 and 3, with a page footer div inserted inside the <ul> element between the first list item and the remaining items. Similarly, the written comments list (Section III) is split across pages 3 and 4 with a footer div and image inserted inside the <ul>. This creates malformed HTML with content interspersed incorrectly, though the actual text content is not duplicated.
findings.evidence:
The
    starting with 'Environmental:' on page 2 has a page-footer div and page 3 figure img inserted between
  • elements before closing
after page 3 content. Same pattern for Section III list spanning pages 3-4.

findings.description: The governor's name in the HTML header shows 'PAT McCrORY' with inconsistent capitalization, while the PDF shows 'PAT McCRORY'. This is a minor text accuracy issue in the header reproduction.

The first image alt text says 'NC Department of Environmental Quality Logo' but the image dimensions are set to width 612pt which suggests it may be a full-page scan rather than just a logo. The alt text is somewhat inaccurate but minor.
findings.evidence: img alt="NC Department of Environmental Quality Logo" style="width: 612.0pt;"
